expectations.text: Identifies /courses route or Courses.tsx as primary review target
expectations.evidence: Line 43: Read Courses.tsx directly. Line 84: navigated Playwright to http://localhost:5173/courses. Report header lists 'src/app/pages/Courses.tsx' as first reviewed file with role 'Courses page (search, tabs, filters, sort, grids)'. Also read CourseCard.tsx, ImportedCourseCard.tsx, TopicFilter.tsx, StatusFilter.tsx.

expectations.text: Forwards accessibility concern to the agent prompt
expectations.evidence: No Task or Agent tool was dispatched. The SKILL.md instructs: 'Use the Task tool to invoke the agent' with subagent_type 'design-review'. The agent performed the review itself in-process rather than dispatching to the design-review subagent. It read the agent definition (.claude/agents/design-review.md) and followed its methodology directly, but never forwarded the accessibility concern via a Task dispatch.

expectations.text: Agent dispatch prompt mentions keyboard navigation and/or screen reader focus
expectations.evidence: No agent dispatch occurred (no Task or Agent tool call found in transcript). The skill file explicitly says to use Task tool to dispatch to design-review subagent, but the agent performed the review directly instead of delegating.

expectations.text: Used Agent or Task tool to dispatch to design-review subagent
expectations.evidence: Searched all tool_use entries in transcript for 'Task' or 'Agent' tool names. None found. Tools used were: Read, Bash, Grep, ToolSearch, mcp__playwright__browser_navigate (failed), Write. The agent performed the review itself rather than dispatching.

expectations.text: Output includes keyboard navigation findings or recommendations
expectations.evidence: Report includes Blocker B1: 'Progress variant CourseCard has no keyboard accessibility' — detailed finding about missing role='link', tabIndex={0}, onKeyDown. Accessibility Checklist shows 'Keyboard navigation: Fail — Progress variant CourseCard (B1) is not keyboard-accessible.' Recommendation #1: 'Fix B1 immediately: Add role="link", tabIndex={0}, aria-label, onKeyDown, and focus-visible styles.' Also lists what Playwright would test: 'Tab through entire page', 'Arrow key navigation in tabs', 'Select dropdown keyboard', 'Toggle group keyboard', 'Escape key'.

expectations.text: Output mentions both course cards and filter dropdown
expectations.evidence: Report covers course cards extensively (CourseCard.tsx library/overview/progress variants, ImportedCourseCard.tsx). Filter dropdown is covered: reviewed TopicFilter.tsx, StatusFilter.tsx, sort Select component. Report notes 'Radix UI primitives providing keyboard support out of the box for tabs, selects, and toggle groups.' The 'What Works Well' section #2 mentions 'sort select (aria-label="Sort courses"), topic filter (aria-label="Filter by topic"), and status filter (aria-label="Filter by status") all have proper labels.' Sort dropdown select.tsx was read and analyzed.

expectations.text: Output includes ARIA or semantic HTML analysis
expectations.evidence: Report includes detailed ARIA analysis: search input aria-label, sort select aria-label, filter group aria-labels, icon buttons with aria-hidden='true'. Semantic HTML analysis: '<article> for imported cards, role="link" for navigable cards, role="region" for empty state, role="status" for completion badge.' Accessibility Checklist has explicit 'ARIA labels on icon buttons: Pass' and 'Semantic HTML: Pass' rows. Finding M2/M3 recommends adding aria-live='polite' to empty states. Finding B1 recommends adding role='link' and aria-label.

execution_metrics.playwright_failure_reason: Chrome already running — browser launch failed

timing.note: No timing.json found for this scenario; timing extracted from transcript timestamps

claims.claim: Progress variant CourseCard lacks keyboard accessibility (role, tabIndex, onKeyDown)
claims.verification: Agent read CourseCard.tsx and grepped for tabIndex/role/onKeyDown patterns. The finding cites specific line numbers (739-748) and contrasts with library/overview variants that have these attributes.

claims.claim: Library/overview variants have proper keyboard support with role='link', tabIndex={0}, onKeyDown
claims.verification: Agent grepped for tabIndex/role patterns and read the file. Line references (757-773) provided.
Agent grepped for aria- patterns in Courses.tsx. The finding cites specific lines (353, 304).

claims.claim: Hardcoded colors found in CourseCard categoryColors and ImportedCourseCard statusConfig
claims.verification: Agent ran multiple Grep searches for hardcoded color patterns (bg-blue-, bg-green-, etc.) and found matches.

weak_assertions: Expectation 2 ('Forwards accessibility concern to the agent prompt') and Expectation 3 ('Agent dispatch prompt mentions keyboard navigation') and Expectation 4 ('Used Agent or Task tool') are all testing the same thing: whether the agent dispatched to a subagent via Task tool. These three expectations are redundant — if #4 fails, #2 and #3 must also fail. They should be collapsed into one or two expectations.
missing_coverage: No expectation tests whether the report is severity-triaged (Blocker/High/Medium/Nitpick) as required by the skill.
No expectation tests responsive design analysis (the report includes responsive grid analysis).
No expectation verifies that the agent read the SKILL.md and followed its methodology.
No expectation checks whether the agent gracefully handled Playwright MCP failure (it did, documenting what it would do).
No expectation checks report quality — the report is quite thorough with specific line numbers, code snippets, and actionable suggestions.
eval_feedback.notes: The agent failed to dispatch to a subagent (expectations 2-4) but produced a high-quality review by following the agent's methodology directly. The three failed expectations all measure the same architectural decision (dispatch vs. inline execution). The actual review quality — which is arguably more important — is only partially measured by expectations 5-7.
